name: Snowplow Data Pipeline Structure
description: Hierarchical structure of Snowplow behavioral data pipeline components from event generation to data warehouse

1_Collection.description: SDKs and libraries that generate and send events to the Snowplow Collector

event_types.PageView: Tracks user navigating to a page
event_types.PagePing: Tracks user staying on a page (engagement)
event_types.StructuredEvent: Five-field categorical event (category, action, label, property, value)
event_types.SelfDescribingEvent: Custom event with a self-describing JSON Schema

1_Collection.output: Raw event payload (GET/POST to Collector endpoint)

2_Collection_Server.description: Receives events from trackers, sets network cookies, forwards to enrichment
2_Collection_Server.output: Raw events in Thrift format to Kinesis/Kafka/PubSub stream

3_Enrichment.description: Validates event schemas, applies enrichments, writes to enriched/bad streams

enrichments.User Agent Parser: Parse browser, OS, device from user agent
enrichments.Weather Enrichment: Add weather context from OpenWeather API

3_Enrichment.schema_validation: Each self-describing event/entity is validated against its Iglu schema registry
3_Enrichment.output: Enriched events in JSON format to enriched stream; failed events to bad stream

4_Storage.description: Load enriched events from the stream into data warehouses

5_Modeling.description: dbt-based data models transform raw event data into analytics-ready tables

DataStructure.description: A JSON Schema defining the shape of a self-describing event or entity

DataProduct.description: A tracking plan grouping related event specifications for a product feature or team

EventSpecification.description: A specific event type with trigger context, schema reference, and implementation notes

SchemaRegistry.description: Iglu schema registry storing and serving JSON Schema definitions for validation
